text:
Purpose: lightweight spreadsheet to track manual handbacks and concierge coverage during short unplanned surges. Use as single-pane to coordinate fast swaps and to record unresolved handoffs for follow-up.

How to use (quick):
- Primary owner: person responsible for the route during the surge window.
- Backup: person to escalate to if primary is overloaded or unreachable.
- Last handback: date the route was returned to normal owner or 'pending'.
- SLA impact: quick flag (none / minor / major) to prioritize.
- Notes: short context, who was paged, and temporary mitigations.
- Triage column: action items for next 24h.

Quick totals (snapshot 2035-04-12): 11 routes listed, 4 pending handbacks, 3 major SLAs flagged.

-- TABLE (paste to spreadsheet or keep as reference) --
Columns: Region | Route ID | Surface | Primary owner | Backup | Last handback | SLA impact | Notes | Active jira

text:
Purpose:
A compact, one-page spreadsheet-style sampler to quickly estimate near-term (1 week) contributor-hours and incremental cost if we need to absorb a 10–30% traffic surge across critical runtime integration touchpoints. Meant for quick cross-team alignment during ad-hoc ops/capacity standups — not a formal capacity model.

How to read: columns are conservative midpoints; surge hours are incremental work above baseline. Cost uses a blended hourly rate where applicable (SWE/Infra: $95/hr, SRE/Oncall: $70/hr, SE/SEs: $85/hr, PM/others: $65/hr).

Table: (rows = teams / ownerships)
Team | Primary Owner | Est weekly baseline hours | SLA priority (H/M/L) | Probable surge? (Y/N) | Surge est hours (10–30%) | Blended $/hr | Est baseline $ | Est surge $ | Notes / blockers

text:
Purpose:
This is a quick, shareable sheet to capture current owner availability windows and a very small 'burn' estimate (hrs/week) for ad-hoc cross-functional touchpoints tied to the upcoming runtime canary. Not intended as a long-term roster — temporary snapshot for the next 6 business days.

Context and assumptions:
- Trigger: unexpected canary capacity oscillation observed 2028-02-13; ops asked for lightweight coverage planning for 2028-02-15 through 2028-02-22.
- Burn estimate = expected weekly triage + follow-ups for this incident lane (hours/week). Very coarse, rounded to nearest half-hour.
- Availability windows are local TZ where possible; prefer PST for cross-team alignment.
- Priority: 1 = must-have coverage, 2 = helpful, 3 = optional standby.

Purpose:
This is a compact, lightly curated synthesis of customer threads we incubated during the last six weeks (Dec 2026 - Jan 2027). Goal is to surface the smallest set of low-to-medium effort probes that could materially reduce customer pain or unblock higher-priority Dedicated/Private deals. This is intentionally bootstrappy: notes, raw quotes, and quick triage rather than polished recommendations.

Snapshot (what we looked at):
- 48 support tickets flagged by CS as latency/consistency-related between 2026-12-01 and 2027-01-10.
- 7 partner-run performance audits (2 hosted API, 5 Dedicated).
- 12 CS call transcripts and three on-site research calls.
- Early telemetry: a non-trivial cohort (approx 12 customers) with repeated short-seq heavy-write patterns (chat systems, code assistants).

Quick summary (topline):

text:
Purpose
---
Short: converge leadership and staff on three prioritized company threads for the coming quarter, draft OKRs tied to measurable signals, and prepare an all-staff forum that surfaces tradeoffs and asks. This is a working pack for exec rehearsal and communications — not the final slide deck.

Context and framing
---
Context: After the Dedicated capacity rollout and early Private deployments, teams report rising operational complexity and mixed signals on unit economics vs. latency targets. The goal for the quarter is to focus the org on patterns that move both reliability and unit-cost simultaneously, while preserving product velocity for SDKs and Console improvements.
Why three threads: we want a clear, memorable set of priorities for an allstaff conversation. Too many bullets dilute commitment; three threads gives focus while enabling cross-functional ownership.

Proposed ...

text:
Purpose:
Quick, ad-hoc table to surface who we loop for short handbacks and a lightweight $/effort sample per owner. Intended for product, SRE, and SEs to decide temporary coverage vs. escalations for next 7 days. Not an SLA—just a working econ sketch.

Notes about approach:
- We bucket owners by likely effort (low/med/high) for a one-week coverage window.
- Dollar sampler is back-of-envelope: assume blended fully-burdened hourly rate = $85/hr for contributor time, 40% probability-of-involvement for med, 80% for high, 20% for low.
- This spreadsheet is intentionally compact — use as tie-breaker when assigning rapid handbacks.

Roster table (rows are manual entries from quick calls):
owner,role,primary-contact,email,proximity-to-sprint,estimated-hours-week,effort-bucket,probability,sample-dollar-week,notes

text:
Purpose:
This is a compact, ops-focused communications and handoff template for small-to-medium Redwood inference rollouts (hotfixes, minor model variants, feature toggles). The doc doubles as: 1) an internal ops checklist for release engineers and SREs and 2) a customer-facing release note + comms snippet that PMs / marketing can copy. Keep short; link to longer release notes when needed.

When to use this template:
- Mini-release or patch window (<= 48h stabilization expectation)
- Model variant swaps, routing policy flips, or control-plane config changes
- Customer-impacting behavior where we provide proactive notes (no full PRD change)

Quick release summary (TOP OF MESSAGE - single-paragraph for customers):
- Title: Rolling update: model routing tweak and latency improvements (non-breaking)
- ETA: 2026-03-18 09:00 PT -> 2026-03-18 11:00 PT (2h window)
- Impact: No downtime expect...

text:
Purpose:
This doc is a lightweight, tactical cookbook for running our Q3-bridge quarter (internal name: "Orbit"). It combines a proposed OKR crosswalk, an all-staff agenda skeleton, operational playbook snippets, and a compact risk/mitigation register for exec review. Intended audience: execs, leads across Eng, Product, GTM, and Ops. Status: working draft — looking for feedback from product-ops, eng-sre, and head of GTM by 2026-03-19.

Executive narrative (1-paragraph):
We are entering a three-month bridge focused on reliability-at-scale while preserving velocity for model onboarding and Dedicated sales. The priority is threefold: (1) harden the serving runtime for predictable tail latency across published models, (2) reduce per-token unit cost on hosted API by 8–12% through batching and caching recommendations, and (3) finalize Private deployment packaging for two pilot customers. Th...

Key risks: Dedicated capacity shortage, a critical regression in runtime perf, and a compliance gap in Private onboarding. title: Alignment Catalyst: Company Momentum Summary & Action Registry (Q2→Q3)
preview:

text:
Purpose:
This playbook describes a compact process to source, evaluate, and onboard contingency / emergency hires from a lightweight internal/external rapid pool. The core goal is to minimize time-to-deploy for critical gaps (eg. incident coverage, short-term feature push, leave coverage) while preserving signal on production readiness and team fit.

Scope:
- Roles: primarily engineering (platform, infra, serving-runtime), SRE, and solutions-engineering contractors; adapt for product or ops roles as needed.
- Timeline target: candidate vetted and accepted within 48 hours of probe start; minimal onboarding to productive contributor within 3–7 days.
- Not for full-time strategic hires unless converted through normal loop.

Purpose:
This is a working template and case workbook for running churn retrospectives (postmortems for lost renewals or early terminations). Use this to capture the timeline, root causes, counterfactuals, and a prioritized action plan that feeds product, onboarding, and GTM. Keep it concise — this lives next to the renewal tracker and should be completed within 7 business days of loss.

How to use this doc (quick):
- Owner: CS lead on the account (AE or CSM) opens the doc within 48h of confirmed loss.
- Fill the TL;DR, timeline, and evidence fields first.
- Run a 30–60 minute internal retro with CS, Solutions Eng, Product PM, and SDR/AE as required.
- Publish a 1-paragraph public summary to the Exec Weekly digest and link to the full workbook.

TL;DR (what’s changed since last update)

- Engineering has fixes in flight (or merged) for the highest-risk issues (retry double-counting, missing region dimension, embeddings batch accounting). We are running “shadow billing compare” to validate deltas before any changes affect invoices.

text:
Purpose:
This working playbook is a single-page, operational guide for rapid assessment and escalation when an account is showing signs of churn risk. Focus is on a repeatable health audit, quantifiable risk scoring, executive escalation criteria, and modular "save plays" the CSM team can mix-and-match.

Status note:
- Drafted by Customer Success Ops (Sophie Martinez).
- Intended to be used for mid-size and enterprise accounts (>$50k ARR).
- This is a working doc: expect TODOs and links to specific tickets.

Quick summary checklist (TL;DR):
- Run health audit (15–30m) -> compute Risk Score.
- If Risk Score >= 65 -> start Tier-1 escalation (CSM + AM + SE).
- If Risk Score >= 85 OR customer verbally signals intent to leave OR legal/billing dispute -> Exec escalation within 48h.
- Apply 1–2 save plays within 72h and track progress in the Escalation Tracker.

Purpose:

Quick proposal for running a small-scale experiment on all-hands format and cadence for Q3. This doc bundles: an OKR-focused agenda draft, proposed exec update script, facilitator checklist, measurement plan, and a lightweight rollback/fallback plan. Goal: reduce meeting time by 25% while increasing perceived alignment score in the 30/60 day pulse.

Context and hypothesis:
- Context: Feedback from the last two town halls shows noise around length, heavy exec monologue, and limited time for cross-team Q&A. Engagement metrics: 38% watch-through in recording, 46% survey response stating "too long".
- Hypothesis: Shorter, modular all-hands (45 minutes) with pre-shared micro-briefs + live highlight panel improves cross-team clarity and enables deeper follow-ups via office-hours.

text:
Purpose and scope:
This working doc captures decisions and operational notes for KMS/HSM/BYOK adoption, key rotation procedures, and how we produce tamper-evident evidence for audits and SIEM ingestion. Audience: security eng, infra, SRE, legal/compliance. Status: working draft, expect iterative updates and small experiments in the next 60 days.

High-level choices (decision log):
- Decision 2025-11-10: Use envelope encryption for all customer data at rest. Root (KEK) stored in managed HSM (cloud provider or dedicated HSM) and used only to wrap/unwrap data encryption keys (DEKs). Rationale: limits exposure of KEKs and allows lightweight rewrap on rotation.
- Decision 2025-11-18: Support BYOK for enterprise customers as a first-class flow (customer uploads key material into a dedicated HSM tenant or uses a wrapped KEK). Rationale: compliance requests and regulated customers required BY...

Purpose:
This doc is a compact, interviewer-friendly rubric + new-hire early-embed shadowing checklist intended for roles with cross-functional impact (infra, platform, SRE, devtools). Goal: reduce decision friction while preserving qualitative evidence capture and give new hires a 2-week shadowing plan that speeds role activation and alignment.

How to use this doc (quick):
- Interviewers: use the 5-dimension rubric below. Score 1-4, add 2–3 sentences of evidence per dimension shown.
- Hiring manager / TA: aggregate scores, read evidence, apply decision matrix. If split, schedule follow-up pair interview focused on the weakest dimension.
- Onboarding partner: once offer accepted, convert evidence notes into a personalized shadow plan.

Purpose:
This playbook defines a 12-week pilot to evaluate "skill-first" hiring for lateral senior ICs (L4-L6 engineering ICs). The pilot flips the usual resume-centric funnel: assess demonstrable day-one skills through short, paid pairing engagements and a compact on-site loop focused on practical scope and handoffs. Goal = faster interviews, clearer quality signal, and a structured micro-onboarding path that maximizes early impact.

Hypothesis:
- Candidates who demonstrate productive contributions in a paid 4-8 hour pairing engagement correlate strongly with successful 30/60/90 outcomes.
- A tighter rubric that weights production-readiness and team-fit over pedigree reduces time-to-offer and lowers false positives.

Scope & constraints:
- Candidate pool: lateral ICs (3+ years experience in relevant stacks) applying to eng teams building inference, runtime, infra, and console feature...

Purpose:
This working doc captures a compact, privacy-conscious candidate skill scorecard plus an explicit handoff agreement for new hires and role transitions. Goal: increase repeatable, transparent signals for hiring decisions and make onboarding handoffs faster (target: cut time-to-productivity by 20% for first 30 days).

Scope and constraints:
- Applies to IC roles (IC-1 through IC-5) and first-line managers across engineering, product and people teams.
- Not a replacement for the formal offer packet or legal onboarding.
- Candidate-facing summaries derived from this scorecard must be redacted of panel-level comments and only surface agreed growth areas and positive highlights.

How to use this doc (quick runbook):
